Minnesota Vikings $15M quarterback tied to Matthew Stafford’s Los Angeles Rams
description: With Kyler Murray and Carson Wentz now in the Minnesota Vikings, J.J. McCarthy’s time with the franchise may be coming to a close. USA TODAY predicts a draft-day trade for McCarthy. ESPN’s Bill Barnwell suggests a trade package sending McCarthy to the Matthew Stafford-led Los Angeles Rams for a higher draft pick. This move could allow McCarthy to develop under head coach Sean McVay. As McVay turned around Baker Mayfield’s career, he could do the same with McCarthy.

Rockets, drones & missiles: Can Hezbollah sustain a full-scale war with Israel?
description: The Israel-Lebanon border is tense as Hezbollah, despite Israeli efforts, demonstrates operational capability and strategic patience. Its dual power structure with the Lebanese Armed Forces and a sophisticated, adaptable arsenal, including extensive small arms, anti-tank systems, and rocket artillery, presents a significant challenge. This resilience shapes Lebanon's military leverage against Israel, creating a volatile balance.

Travis Kelce’s ring for Taylor Swift sparks antique diamond trend
description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have reignited interest in antique diamonds, shifting focus from flawless, mass-produced stones to rare, character-rich pieces. Their engagement spotlighted vintage craftsmanship, sustainability, and uniqueness. Jewellers report rising demand, though high-quality antique diamonds remain scarce and costly. As buyers seek meaning over perfection, these historic stones are becoming prized choices, blending romance, heritage, and individuality in modern engagement trends.

title: Floyd Mayweather hit with $100k lawsuit amid $7million tax debt
description: Boxing legend Floyd Mayweather Jr is once again in legal trouble after a company accused him of not paying for private jet services. A complaint filed in Los Angeles County claims that Jet Set Aircraft Inc. is owed more than $105,000. The case is linked to a private charter flight in 2025. The lawsuit also names Mayweather’s company, TBE Aviation, and other individuals who were allegedly involved in arranging the trip.
